<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # EPAM CLI Orchestration Script - AI-driven development loop
4: # This script orchestrates Claude Code CLI for autonomous story implementation
5: #
6: # Usage:
7: #   ./claude.sh                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
8: #   ./claude.sh --phase phase1       # Implement all stories in a phase
9: #   ./claude.sh --list-phases        # Show available phases
10: #   ./claude.sh US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
11: #   ./claude.sh --dry-run            # Show what would be implemented
12: #   ./claude.sh --status             # Show current PRD status
13: #   ./claude.sh --interactive        # Run with permission prompts (safer)
14: #
15: # Note: By default, runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions for autonomous operation.
16: #       Use --interactive flag if you want to approve each file operation.
17: 
18: set -e
19: 
20: SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
21: AUTOMATION_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
22: PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$AUTOMATION_DIR")"
23: PRD_FILE="${PRD_FILE:-$AUTOMATION_DIR/prd.json}"
24: LOG_DIR="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs"
25: PROGRESS_LOG="$LOG_DIR/progress.txt"
26: AGENTS_FILE="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/AGENTS.md"
27: CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR="$LOG_DIR/claude_outputs"
28: AGENT_PROFILES_FILE="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/profiles.json"
29: MONITOR_STATUS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/agent-status.json"
30: 
31: # Worktree configuration (set by --worktree flag)
32: WORKTREE_MODE=""        # "primary", "independent", or "" for main
33: MAIN_PRD_FILE=""        # Points to main repo's prd.json when in worktree mode
34: REVIEW_PHASE=""         # Phase name for --review-phase mode
35: CURRENT_PHASE=""        # Current phase being executed (for cost tracking)
36: 
37: # Configuration
38: CLAUDE_CMD="${CLAUDE_CMD:-claude}"  # Allow override via environment
39: MAX_RETRIES=2
40: RETRY_DELAY=5
41: # Orchestration mode — inherited from run-agent-orchestration.sh or set directly
42: ORCH_MODE="${ORCH_MODE:-bash}"
43: 
44: # Effort -> model + max-turns mapping
45: # Stories carry an optional "effort" field: low | medium (default) | high
46: # These map to a model and a max-turns cap for the Claude CLI invocation.
47: EFFORT_MODEL_LOW="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
48: EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM="claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
49: EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH="claude-opus-4-6"
50: 
51: # resolve_effort_settings <story_id>
52: # Sets STORY_MODEL and STORY_MAX_TURNS globals based on story's effort field.
53: resolve_effort_settings() {
54:     local story_id="$1"
55:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
56:     local effort
57:     effort=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
58:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .effort // "medium"' \
59:         "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "medium")
60: 
61:     case "$effort" in
62:         low)
63:             STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_LOW"
64:             STORY_MAX_TURNS=10
65:             ;;
66:         high)
67:             STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_HIGH"
68:             STORY_MAX_TURNS=""
69:             ;;
70:         *)  # medium (default)
71:             STORY_MODEL="$EFFORT_MODEL_MEDIUM"
72:             STORY_MAX_TURNS=30
73:             ;;
74:     esac
75:     log "  Effort[$effort] -> model=$(basename $STORY_MODEL) turns=${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-unlimited}"
76: }
77: 
78: # resolve_provider_settings <story_id>
79: # Reads aiProvider from the story and sets STORY_PROVIDER global.
80: # Values: claude-sonnet | claude-opus | opencode | codex | epam (default: claude-sonnet)
81: resolve_provider_settings() {
82:     local story_id="$1"
83:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
84:     STORY_PROVIDER=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
85:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .aiProvider // "claude-sonnet"' \
86:         "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "claude-sonnet")
87:     log "  Provider[$STORY_PROVIDER] -> CLI=$(provider_to_cli "$STORY_PROVIDER")"
88: }
89: 
90: # provider_to_cli <provider>
91: # Returns the CLI binary name for a given aiProvider value.
92: provider_to_cli() {
93:     case "$1" in
94:         opencode)       echo "opencode" ;;
95:         codex)          echo "codex" ;;
96:         codemie-claude) echo "codemie-claude" ;;
97:         epam)           echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # epam: treat same as claude for now
98:         *)              echo "$CLAUDE_CMD" ;;  # claude-sonnet, claude-opus, etc.
99:     esac
100: }
101: 
102: # normalize_provider_json <provider> <raw_jsonl_file> <out_json_file>
103: # Converts provider-specific JSONL output into a normalized JSON object
104: # matching Claude's format: {result, total_cost_usd, usage.{input_tokens,output_tokens}}
105: normalize_provider_json() {
106:     local provider="$1"
107:     local raw_file="$2"
108:     local out_file="$3"
109:     case "$provider" in
110:         opencode)
111:             # OpenCode emits JSONL stream; try step_finish first, then fall back to any cost/usage field
112:             local sf_line
113:             sf_line=$(grep '"type":"step_finish"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
114:             if [ -z "$sf_line" ]; then
115:                 sf_line=$(grep -E '"cost"|"total_cost"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1 || echo '{}')
116:             fi
117:             sf_line="${sf_line:-{\}}"
118:             # Extract text parts for result summary
119:             local result_text
120:             result_text=$(grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
121:                 | jq -rs '[.[].part.text // .[].text // ""] | join("")' 2>/dev/null || echo "opencode run completed")
122:             jq -n \
123:                 --arg rt "$result_text" \
124:                 --argjson sf "$sf_line" \
125:                 '{result: $rt,
126:                   total_cost_usd: ($sf.cost // $sf.part.cost // $sf.total_cost // 0),
127:                   usage: {
128:                       input_tokens:  ($sf.tokens.input  // $sf.part.tokens.input  // $sf.usage.input_tokens  // 0),
129:                       output_tokens: ($sf.tokens.output // $sf.part.tokens.output // $sf.usage.output_tokens // 0)
130:                   }}' > "$out_file" 2>/dev/null
131:             ;;
132:         codex)
133:             # Codex emits JSONL stream; turn.completed has usage (no cost field)
134:             local tc_line
135:             tc_line=$(grep '"type":"turn.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
136:             tc_line="${tc_line:-{\}}"
137:             local result_text
138:             result_text=$(grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
139:                 | jq -rs '[.[].item.text // ""] | join("")' 2>/dev/null || echo "codex exec completed")
140:             jq -n \
141:                 --arg rt "$result_text" \
142:                 --argjson tc "$tc_line" \
143:                 '{result: $rt,
144:                   total_cost_usd: 0,
145:                   usage: {
146:                       input_tokens:  ($tc.usage.input_tokens  // 0),
147:                       output_tokens: ($tc.usage.output_tokens // 0)
148:                   }}' > "$out_file" 2>/dev/null
149:             ;;
150:         codemie-claude)
151:             # codemie-claude: same output format as Claude — nothing to normalize
152:             ;;
153:         epam)
154:             # epam: same output format as Claude — nothing to normalize
155:             ;;
156:         *)
157:             # Claude: already emits normalized JSON; nothing to do
158:             ;;
159:     esac
160: }
161: 
162: # Claude CLI permission flags
163: # These allow Claude to read/write files and execute commands without prompting
164: CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=(
165:     "--dangerously-skip-permissions"  # Skip all permission prompts for autonomous operation
166: )
167: 
168: # Alternative: Use granular permissions (uncomment if preferred over skip-permissions)
169: # CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS=(
170: #     "--allowedTools" "Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep,Bash"
171: # )
172: 
173: # Colors for output
174: RED='\033[0;31m'
175: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
176: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
177: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
178: CYAN='\033[0;36m'
179: MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
180: WHITE='\033[1;37m'
181: NC='\033[0m' # No Color
182: 
183: # Logging functions
184: log() {
185:     echo -e "${BLUE}[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')]${NC} $1"
186:     echo "[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
187: }
188: 
189: error() {
190:     echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1" >&2
191:     echo "[ERROR] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
192: }
193: 
194: success() {
195:     echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
196:     echo "[SUCCESS] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
197: }
198: 
199: warning() {
200:     echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
201:     echo "[WARNING] [$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "$PROGRESS_LOG"
202: }
203: 
204: info() {
205:     echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
206: }
207: 
208: # Initialize directories and logs
209: initialize() {
210:     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$PROGRESS_LOG")"
211:     mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR"
212: 
213:     if [ ! -f "$PROGRESS_LOG" ]; then
214:         cat > "$PROGRESS_LOG" << EOF
215: === EPAM CLI Orchestration Progress Log ===
216: Started: $(date)
217: Project: $(jq -r '.project.name // "Unknown"' "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
218: ==========================================
219: 
220: EOF
221:     fi
222: }
223: 
224: # Check prerequisites
225: check_prerequisites() {
226:     # Check for jq
227:     if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
228:         error "jq is required but not installed. Install with: sudo apt install jq"
229:         exit 1
230:     fi
231: 
232:     # Check for Claude CLI
233:     if ! command -v "$CLAUDE_CMD" &> /dev/null; then
234:         error "Claude CLI not found. Expected command: $CLAUDE_CMD"
235:         error "Install Claude Code CLI or set CLAUDE_CMD environment variable"

252: }

328:     local estimated_hours dep_count plan_flag
329:     estimated_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
330:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
331:     dep_count=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
332:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | (.dependencies // []) | length' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
333:     plan_flag=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
334:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .technicalNotes.planModeRequired // false' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo false)
335: 
336:     [ "$plan_flag" = "true" ] && return 0
337:     # Use awk for float comparison (avoids bc dependency for this check)
338:     awk -v h="$estimated_hours" 'BEGIN{exit !(h >= 6)}' && return 0
339:     [ "${dep_count:-0}" -ge 2 ] && return 0
340:     return 1
341: }

344: # run_plan_mode <story_id>
345: # Invokes Claude in planning mode to produce execution-ready artifacts.
346: # Posts a plan_summary message to agent-messages.jsonl when complete.

348: run_plan_mode() {
349:     local story_id="$1"
350:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
351:     local plan_log="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_plan_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
352:     local plan_json="${plan_log%.log}_result.json"
353:     local messages_jsonl="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-messages.jsonl"
354: 
355:     local agent_role
356:     agent_role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
357:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
358: 
359:     local plan_prompt
360:     plan_prompt=$(cat << PLAN_PROMPT_EOF
361: You are a planning agent. Produce an execution-ready plan for story ${story_id} BEFORE implementation begins.
362: 
363: ## Required Outputs
364: 1. Implementation steps with target file paths
365: 2. Dependency validation (each dep: satisfied yes/no, reason)
366: 3. Risk register (top 3 risks + mitigations)
367: 4. Test plan (new tests required + regression scope)
368: 5. Acceptance criteria mapping (each criterion -> implementation step)
369: 6. Cost/effort forecast (confirm or adjust estimatedHours)
370: 
371: ## On Completion
372: Append a single-line JSON record to orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl:
373: {
374:   "id":"plan_${story_id}_\$(date +%s)",
375:   "timestamp":"\$(date -Iseconds)",
376:   "from_agent":"plan-agent",
377:   "to_agent":"${agent_role}",
378:   "story_id":"${story_id}",
379:   "phase_id":"${CURRENT_PHASE:-unknown}",
380:   "message_type":"plan_summary",
381:   "priority":"normal",
382:   "subject":"Plan ready for ${story_id}",
383:   "body":"<one-sentence summary of key risks/steps>",
384:   "status":"new"
385: }
386: Write it atomically: (flock -w 10 9 >> orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl; printf '%s\n' '<json>' >&9) 9>>orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl
387: 
388: ## Story to Plan
389: Read orchestrations/prd.json for story ${story_id} full details, then produce the plan above.
390: PLAN_PROMPT_EOF
391:     )
392: 
393:     log "Plan mode: generating execution plan for $story_id..."
394:     touch "$messages_jsonl"
395:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
396:     if echo "$plan_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
397:             "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" 2>/dev/null > "$plan_json"; then
398:         jq -r '.result // empty' "$plan_json" 2>/dev/null >> "$plan_log" || true
399:         success "Plan mode completed for $story_id — see $plan_log"
400:     else
401:         warning "Plan mode failed for $story_id — continuing with direct implementation"
402:     fi
403: }

406: # post_completion_message <story_id> <status>
407: # Appends a status message to agent-messages.jsonl after each story run.
408: # Only writes when ORCH_MODE=hybrid OR the bus file already exists.
409: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
410: post_completion_message() {
411:     local story_id="$1"
412:     local status="$2"   # "completed" | "failed"
413:     local messages_jsonl="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/agent-messages.jsonl"
414:     local lock_file="${messages_jsonl}.lock"
415: 
416:     # Skip if bash-only mode and bus hasn't been seeded yet
417:     if [ "${ORCH_MODE:-bash}" != "hybrid" ] && [ ! -f "$messages_jsonl" ]; then
418:         return 0
419:     fi
420: 
421:     local prd_target="${MAIN_PRD_FILE:-$PRD_FILE}"
422:     local agent_role
423:     agent_role=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" \
424:         '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .agentRole // "unknown"' "$prd_target" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
425: 
426:     local phase_id="${CURRENT_PHASE:-unknown}"
427:     local timestamp
428:     timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)
429:     local epoch
430:     epoch=$(date +%s)
431:     local msg_id="status_${story_id}_${epoch}"
432: 
433:     local message
434:     message=$(jq -cn \
435:         --arg id      "$msg_id" \
436:         --arg ts      "$timestamp" \
437:         --arg from    "$agent_role" \
438:         --arg to      "orchestrator" \
439:         --arg sid     "$story_id" \
440:         --arg pid     "$phase_id" \
441:         --arg subj    "Story $story_id $status" \
442:         --arg body    "Story $story_id finished with status: $status" \
443:         --arg sstatus "$status" \
444:         '{
445:             id: $id, timestamp: $ts,
446:             from_agent: $from, to_agent: $to,
447:             story_id: $sid, phase_id: $pid,
448:             message_type: "status",
449:             priority: "normal",
450:             subject: $subj, body: $body,
451:             story_status: $sstatus,
452:             status: "new"
453:         }')
454: 
455:     touch "$messages_jsonl"
456:     (
457:         flock -w 10 200 || return 0
458:         echo "$message" | jq -c '.' >> "$messages_jsonl"
459:     ) 200>"$lock_file"
460: }

517: # Get prioritized list of incomplete stories (respects phases, dependencies, priority)
518: get_prioritized_stories() {
519:     local result=()
520: 
521:     # Get phases in order
522:     local phases=$(get_phases)
523: 
524:     if [ -z "$phases" ]; then
525:         # No phases defined, fall back to all incomplete stories sorted by priority
526:         jq -r '.stories[] | select(.completed == false) | "\(.priority // "medium")|\(.id)"' "$PRD_FILE" | \
527:             sort -t'|' -k1,1 | cut -d'|' -f2
528:         return
529:     fi
530: 
531:     # Process each phase in order
532:     while IFS= read -r phase; do
533:         [ -z "$phase" ] && continue
534: 
535:         # Get stories in this phase
536:         local phase_stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")
537: 
538:         # For each story in the phase, check if it's incomplete and dependencies are met
539:         while IFS= read -r story_id; do
540:             [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
541: 
542:             if ! is_story_completed "$story_id" && are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
543:                 echo "$story_id"
544:             fi
545:         done <<< "$phase_stories"
546:     done <<< "$phases"

554: # List available phases with status
555: list_phases() {
556:     echo ""
557:     echo -e "${MAGENTA}=== Implementation Phases ===${NC}"

560:     local phases=$(get_phases)
561: 
562:     if [ -z "$phases" ]; then
563:         echo -e "${YELLOW}No phases defined in implementationOrder${NC}"
564:         return
565:     fi
566: 
567:     while IFS= read -r phase; do
568:         [ -z "$phase" ] && continue
569: 
570:         local total=0
571:         local completed=0
572:         local stories=$(get_phase_stories "$phase")
573: 
574:         while IFS= read -r story_id; do
575:             [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
576:             total=$((total + 1))
577:             if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
578:                 completed=$((completed + 1))
579:             fi
580:         done <<< "$stories"
581: 
582:         local status_color=$YELLOW
583:         local status_icon="o"
584:         if [ $completed -eq $total ] && [ $total -gt 0 ]; then
585:             status_color=$GREEN
586:             status_icon="+"
587:         elif [ $completed -gt 0 ]; then
588:             status_color=$CYAN
589:             status_icon="~"
590:         fi
591: 
592:         echo -e "${status_color}${status_icon}${NC} ${WHITE}$phase${NC} ($completed/$total completed)"
593: 
594:         # Show stories in phase
595:         while IFS= read -r story_id; do
596:             [ -z "$story_id" ] && continue
597:             local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
598:             if is_story_completed "$story_id"; then
599:                 echo -e "    ${GREEN}+${NC} $story_id: $title"
600:             else
601:                 local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
602:                 local deps_info=""
603:                 if [ -n "$deps" ]; then
604:                     if are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
605:                         deps_info=" ${CYAN}(deps: $deps)${NC}"
606:                     else
607:                         deps_info=" ${RED}(blocked by: $deps)${NC}"
608:                     fi
609:                 fi
610:                 echo -e "    ${YELLOW}o${NC} $story_id: $title$deps_info"
611:             fi
612:         done <<< "$stories"
613:         echo ""
614:     done <<< "$phases"
615: }

698: # Invoke Claude CLI to implement a story
699: implement_story() {
700:     local story_id=$1
701:     local retry_count=0
702:     local output_file="$CLAUDE_OUTPUT_DIR/${story_id}_$(date +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S').log"
703:     local story_started_at=$(date -Iseconds)
704: 
705:     local title=$(get_story_title "$story_id")
706:     log "Implementing story: $story_id - $title"
707:     update_monitor_status "start" "$story_id"
708: 
709:     # Check dependencies first
710:     if ! are_dependencies_satisfied "$story_id"; then
711:         local deps=$(get_story_dependencies "$story_id" | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//')
712:         error "Cannot implement $story_id - dependencies not satisfied: $deps"
713:         return 1
714:     fi
715: 
716:     # Plan mode check: run planning agent before implementation if complexity thresholds met
717:     if check_plan_mode_required "$story_id"; then
718:         log "Plan mode required for $story_id (estimatedHours>=6, deps>=2, or flag set)"
719:         run_plan_mode "$story_id"
720:     fi
721: 
722:     # Resolve effort -> model + max-turns for this story (stable across retries)
723:     resolve_effort_settings "$story_id"
724:     local model_flag=()
725:     local turns_flag=()
726:     [ -n "${STORY_MODEL:-}" ]     && model_flag=(--model "$STORY_MODEL")
727:     [ -n "${STORY_MAX_TURNS:-}" ] && turns_flag=(--max-turns "$STORY_MAX_TURNS")
728: 
729:     # Resolve aiProvider -> which CLI binary to use
730:     resolve_provider_settings "$story_id"
731:     local story_cli
732:     story_cli=$(provider_to_cli "${STORY_PROVIDER:-claude-sonnet}")
733: 
734:     while [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
735:         # Rebuild prompt each attempt: retry_count and KB ID must reflect current state
736:         local next_kb_id
737:         next_kb_id=$(get_next_kb_id)
738:         local prompt
739:         prompt="$(build_implementation_prompt "$story_id")
740: $(build_kb_prompt_section "$story_id" "$retry_count" "$next_kb_id")"
741: 
742:         # Log the prompt
743:         echo "=== Prompt for $story_id (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
744:         echo "$prompt" >> "$output_file"
745:         echo "=== End Prompt ===" >> "$output_file"
746:         echo "" >> "$output_file"
747: 
748:         log "Invoking $story_cli (attempt $((retry_count + 1))/$((MAX_RETRIES + 1)))..."
749: 
750:         # Change to project root for the CLI to have correct context
751:         cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
752: 
753:         echo "=== $story_cli Output (attempt $((retry_count + 1))) ===" >> "$output_file"
754: 
755:         local json_result_file="${output_file%.log}_result.json"
756:         local invoke_success=false
757: 
758:         case "${STORY_PROVIDER:-claude-sonnet}" in
759:             opencode)
760:                 # OpenCode: pass prompt via temp file (prompts can exceed arg limits)
761:                 # --format json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
762:                 local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
763:                 local prompt_file="${json_result_file%.json}_prompt.txt"
764:                 echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
765:                 if opencode run --format json "$(cat "$prompt_file")" \
766:                         > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
767:                     normalize_provider_json "opencode" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
768:                     # Append text output to log
769:                     grep '"type":"text"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
770:                         | jq -r '.part.text // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
771:                     invoke_success=true
772:                 fi
773:                 rm -f "$prompt_file"
774:                 ;;
775:             codex)
776:                 # Codex: reads prompt from stdin when '-' is passed
777:                 # --json emits JSONL stream; we normalize it after
778:                 local raw_file="${json_result_file%.json}_raw.jsonl"
779:                 if echo "$prompt" | codex exec --json - \
780:                         > "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null; then
781:                     normalize_provider_json "codex" "$raw_file" "$json_result_file"
782:                     # Append text output to log
783:                     grep '"type":"item.completed"' "$raw_file" 2>/dev/null \
784:                         | jq -r '.item.text // empty' 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || true
785:                     invoke_success=true
786:                 fi
787:                 ;;
788:             codemie-claude)
789:                 # codemie-claude: same invocation pattern as claude — --print --output-format json
790:                 if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE codemie-claude --print --output-format json \
791:                         "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
792:                         2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
793:                     invoke_success=true
794:                 fi
795:                 ;;
796:             epam)
797:                 # epam: treat same as claude — same CLI, same output format
798:                 if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
799:                         "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
800:                         2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
801:                     invoke_success=true
802:                 fi
803:                 ;;
804:             *)
805:                 # Claude (claude-sonnet, claude-opus, or any claude-* value)
806:                 # --print --output-format json captures cost+tokens in a single JSON object
807:                 if echo "$prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --print --output-format json \
808:                         "${model_flag[@]}" "${turns_flag[@]}" "${CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS[@]}" \
809:                         2>/dev/null > "$json_result_file"; then
810:                     invoke_success=true
811:                 fi
812:                 ;;
813:         esac
814: 
815:         if [ "$invoke_success" = true ]; then
816:             # Extract human-readable result text and append to output log
817:             if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
818:                 jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
819:             fi
820:             echo "" >> "$output_file"
821:             echo "=== End $story_cli Output ===" >> "$output_file"
822:             success "$story_cli completed implementation for $story_id"
823:             update_monitor_status "complete" "$story_id" "Implementation succeeded"
824:             append_cost_record "$story_id" "completed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
825:             post_completion_message "$story_id" "completed"
826:             return 0
827:         else
828:             local exit_code=$?
829:             # Still capture any partial JSON output
830:             if [ -f "$json_result_file" ]; then
831:                 jq -r '.result // empty' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null >> "$output_file" || cat "$json_result_file" >> "$output_file"
832:             fi
833:             echo "" >> "$output_file"
834:             echo "=== $story_cli exited with code $exit_code ===" >> "$output_file"
835: 
836:             retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
837:             if [ $retry_count -le $MAX_RETRIES ]; then
838:                 warning "$story_cli failed, retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s..."
839:                 sleep $RETRY_DELAY
840:             fi
841:         fi
842:     done
843: 
844:     error "Failed to implement $story_id after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
845:     update_monitor_status "fail" "$story_id" "Failed after $((MAX_RETRIES + 1)) attempts"
846:     append_cost_record "$story_id" "failed" "$story_started_at" "$(date -Iseconds)" "$output_file" "$json_result_file"
847:     post_completion_message "$story_id" "failed"
848:     return 1
849: }

854:     local status=$2  # "completed" or "failed"
855:     local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)

863:

868:             success "Story $story_id marked as completed"

890:     local cost_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/logs/phase-cost.jsonl"

893:     # Read story metadata from prd.json

895:     local title=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .title // "unknown"' "$prd_target")

897:     local forecast_hours=$(jq -r --arg id "$story_id" '.stories[] | select(.id == $id) | .estimatedHours // 0' "$prd_target")
898:     local phase_id="${CURRENT_PHASE:-}"
899:     if [ -z "$phase_id" ]; then

907:     # Compute elapsed minutes

910:     local elapsed_minutes=0

912:         elapsed_minutes=$(echo "scale=2; ($end_epoch - $start_epoch) / 60" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
913:     fi

915:     # Parse cost/token/turn usage from Claude CLI JSON result (--output-format json)
916:     local tokens_in=0 tokens_out=0 cost_usd=0 task_turns=0

918:         cost_usd=$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
919:         tokens_in=$(jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
920:         tokens_out=$(jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
921:         # Turn count: Claude CLI may report num_turns or turns
922:         task_turns=$(jq -r '.num_turns // .turns // .usage.turns // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
923:         # Also capture cache tokens if present
924:         local cache_create=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
925:         local cache_read=$(jq -r '.usage.cache_read_input_tokens // 0' "$json_result_file" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo 0)
926:         # Total input includes base + cache tokens
927:         tokens_in=$(( ${tokens_in:-0} + ${cache_create:-0} + ${cache_read:-0} ))
928:     fi
929:     [ -z "$tokens_in" ] && tokens_in=0
930:     [ -z "$tokens_out" ] && tokens_out=0
931:     [ -z "$cost_usd" ] && cost_usd=0
932:     [ -z "$task_turns" ] && task_turns=0

937:         jq -cn \

939:             --arg sid "$story_id" --arg st "$title" \
940:             --arg aid "$agent_id" --arg an "$agent_id" \
941:             --argjson fh "${forecast_hours:-0}" --argjson fc 0 \

#!/bin/bash

# EPAM CLI Orchestration Script - AI-driven development loop
# This script orchestrates Claude Code CLI for autonomous story implementation
#
# Usage:
#   ./claude.sh                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
#   ./claude.sh --phase phase1       # Implement all stories in a phase
#   ./claude.sh --list-phases        # Show available phases
#   ./claude.sh US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
#   ./claude.sh --dry-run            # Show what would be implemented
#   ./claude.sh --status             # Show current PRD status
#   ./claude.sh --interactive        # Run with permission prompts (safer)
#
# Note: By default, runs with --dangerously-skip-permissions for autonomous operation.
#       Use --interactive flag if you want to approve each file operation.

set -e

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

26: │   ├── check-phase-gate.sh          # Phase gate enforcement
27: │   ├── team-lead-review.sh          # Team Lead code review cycle
28: │   ├── worktree-health-check.sh     # Worktree commit verification
29: │   ├── merge-worktree.sh            # Worktree merge back to main
30: │   ├── check-dependencies.sh        # Story dependency graph check
31: │   ├── send-message.sh              # Agent message bus (hybrid mode)
32: │   ├── receive-messages.sh          # Read messages from bus
33: │   ├── list-messages.sh             # List bus messages
34: │   ├── ack-message.sh               # Acknowledge message
35: │   ├── sync-monitor-stories.sh      # Sync story data to monitor
36: │   ├── load-phase-graph.sh          # Load phase graph to Neo4j
37: │   ├── load-phase-graph.py          # Python Neo4j loader
38: │   ├── update-cost-forecasts.sh     # Update cost forecast data
39: │   ├── code-review-cycle.sh         # Code review automation
40: │   └── reset-cost-test.sh           # Reset cost data for testing
41: ├── logs/
42: │   ├── agent-status.json            # Live monitor data (read by dashboards)
43: │   ├── phase-cost.jsonl             # Per-story cost/time records
44: │   ├── phase-skill-assessments.jsonl
45: │   ├── agent-messages.jsonl         # MCP message bus (hybrid mode)
46: │   ├── profiles-audit.jsonl         # Pre-phase skill augmentation log
47: │   └── phase-improvements/          # Per-phase improvement reports
48: └── dashboards/
49:     ├── monitor.html                 # Live lane/story status
50:     ├── prd-viewer.html              # All stories + filters
51:     ├── agent-profiles.html          # Agent profiles + skills
52:     ├── orchestration-plan.html      # Architecture overview
53:     ├── phase-cost-monitor.html      # Cost tracking + variance
54:     ├── agent-messages.html          # Message bus viewer
55:     └── agents-orchestration.html   # Orchestration flow diagram
56: ```
57: 
58: ---
59: 
60: ## Quick Start
61: 
62: ```bash
63: # Run a phase (bash mode — default)
64: ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops
65: 
66: # Preview execution plan without running
67: ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops --dry-run
68: 
69: # Run hybrid mode (coordination agent + message bus)
70: ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase multi_agent --mode hybrid
71: 
72: # Run specific story
73: ./orchestrations/scripts/claude.sh EPAM-001
74: 
75: # View dashboards (requires agent-monitor Docker service)
76: docker compose -f docker-compose.epam-cli.yml up agent-monitor -d
77: # Then open: http://localhost:8092/monitor.html
78: ```
79: 
80: ---
81: 
82: ## Phases
83: 
84: | Phase | Mode | Stories | Priority | Description |
85: |-------|------|---------|----------|-------------|
86: | `health_check` | bash | EPAM-HC-001 to HC-004 | P0 | Pre-flight CLI validation |
87: | `finops` | bash | EPAM-001 to EPAM-003 | P1 | Budget guardrails + burn-up reports |
88: | `agent_intelligence` | bash | EPAM-004 to EPAM-008 | P1 | Gold profiles, /plan mode, decision records, MCP |
89: | `team_features` | bash | EPAM-009 to EPAM-011 | P2 | /replay, shared team memory |
90: | `multi_agent` | hybrid | EPAM-012 to EPAM-014 | P3 | Ralph Wiggum loop, Squad execution |
91: | `enterprise` | hybrid | EPAM-015 to EPAM-018 | P3 | Remote constraints, auditors, marketplace |
92: | `rag_poc` | bash | EPAM-019 to EPAM-020 | P4 | RAG Asset Discovery POC |
93: 
94: ---
95: 
96: ## Agent Roles
97: 
98: | Role | Responsibilities |
99: |------|-----------------|
100: | `typescript-engineer` | Feature implementation in TypeScript (src/) |
101: | `agent-systems-engineer` | Agent orchestration, multi-agent coordination, MCP |
102: | `billing-engineer` | FinOps, cost tracking, budget guardrails |
103: | `cli-ux-engineer` | REPL commands, slash commands, terminal UX |
104: | `test-engineer` | vitest unit + integration tests |
105: | `team-lead-agent` | Phase gates, dependency validation, code review authority |
106: | `review-agent` | Code review per story (TypeScript quality, test coverage) |
107: 
108: ---
109: 
110: ## Orchestration Pipeline
111: 
112: ```
113: Step 0.5  Pre-phase skill assessment (augments agent profiles)
114: Step 0.6  [hybrid only] Pre-phase coordination + message bus seeding
115: Step 1    Main-branch stories (sequential)
116: Step 2    Create git worktrees (if primary/independent stories exist)
117: Step 3    Parallel: PRIMARY + INDEPENDENT lanes run concurrently
118: Step 3.1  Worktree health check + auto-commit
119: Step 3.5  Post-parallel skill assessment
120: Step 3.6  Team Lead code review
121: Step 4    Review stories
122: Step 4.5  Unit test gate: vitest run + tsc --noEmit (blocking)
123: Step 4.8  Pre-gate worktree verification
124: Step 5    Phase gate: cost variance check (ok / warn / escalate)
125: Step 5.5  Interstitial E2E phase (if <phase>_e2e exists in prd.json)
126: Step 6    Final post-phase assessment
127: ```
128: 
129: ---
130: 
131: ## Provider Support
132: 
133: Set `CLAUDE_CMD` to switch AI provider:
134: 
135: ```bash
136: # Claude (default)
137: ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops
138: 
139: # OpenCode
140: CLAUDE_CMD=opencode ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops
141: 
142: # Codex
143: CLAUDE_CMD=codex ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops
144: 
145: # Codemie-Claude
146: CLAUDE_CMD=codemie-claude ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase health_check
147: 
148: # EPAM CLI (once built — dogfooding)
149: CLAUDE_CMD="node dist/epam.js run" ./orchestrations/scripts/run-agent-orchestration.sh --phase finops
150: ```
151: 
152: ---
153: 
154: ## Future: CLI Integration
155: 
156: Once epam-cli is mature, the orchestration mechanism will be injected into the CLI as a first-class feature:
157: 
158: ```bash
159: epam orchestrate --phase finops          # Run a phase
160: epam orchestrate --dry-run               # Preview
161: epam orchestrate status                  # Show phase/story status
162: epam orchestrate story EPAM-001          # Run single story
163: ```
164: 
165: This transforms epam-cli from an AI assistant into a full **autonomous development orchestrator** — using itself to build itself.

(End of file - total 165 lines)

# EPAM CLI — Agent Orchestration

Multi-agent orchestration system for building epam-cli features autonomously using Claude Code (and other AI CLIs) with parallel execution, phase gates, cost tracking, and live dashboards.

Based on the fitnessapp/shadow-play orchestration pattern. Supports: **claude**, **codex**, **opencode**, **codemie-claude**, and (future) **epam** itself.

> For full context-loading instructions, see [INSTRUCTIONS.md](./INSTRUCTIONS.md).

orchestrations/
├── prd.json                    # Master story definitions + phase assignments
├── README.md                   # This file
├── agents/
│   ├── profiles.json           # 7 agent role definitions (rich system prompts)
│   ├── AGENTS.md               # Auto-generated learned patterns log
│   └── KB.md                   # Shared knowledge base

384: run_pre_phase_assessment() {
385:     local phase_id=$1
386:     local profiles_file="$AUTOMATION_DIR/agents/profiles.json"
387:     local profiles_backup="${profiles_file}.original"
388:     local profiles_audit="$LOG_DIR/profiles-audit.jsonl"
389:     local assessment_log="$LOG_DIR/pre-assessment-${phase_id}.log"
390: 
391:     touch "$profiles_audit"
392: 
393:     # Backup profiles.json on first pre-phase run (idempotent)
394:     if [ ! -f "$profiles_backup" ]; then
395:         cp "$profiles_file" "$profiles_backup"
396:         log "Backed up original profiles to $profiles_backup"
397:     fi
398: 
399:     log "Running pre-phase skill assessment for '$phase_id'..."
400: 
401:     # Build assessment prompt
402:     local assessment_prompt
403:     assessment_prompt=$(cat << PROMPT_HEADER
404: You are the skill assessment agent running in PRE-PHASE mode. Your job is to detect skill gaps in agent profiles BEFORE the phase runs, and augment profiles with missing knowledge.
405: 
406: ## Task
407: 1. Read ${PRD_REL} and find the stories in the current phase's implementationOrder
408: 2. For each story, extract required skills from description + technicalNotes (especially technicalNotes.requiredSkills)
409: 3. Read orchestrations/agents/profiles.json and find the profile for each story's agentRole
410: 4. Compare: does the agent's profile text mention each required skill?
411: 5. For any GAPS found:
412:    a. Append a sentence to the agent's profile in profiles.json mentioning the missing skill
413:    b. Append a JSONL record to orchestrations/logs/profiles-audit.jsonl:
414:       {"timestamp":"<ISO8601>","phase_id":"<phase>","agent_role":"<role>","event":"skill_added","skill":"<skill>","skill_category":"<category>","context":"Story <id> requires <skill>","added_by":"pre-phase-assessment"}
415:    c. Use flock when writing to JSONL files
416: 6. Write a summary to orchestrations/logs/phase-improvements/pre-<phase_id>.md
417: 
418: Known skill categories: deployment_platform, language, framework, testing, database, infrastructure, api, cloud_service
419: 
420: IMPORTANT: Keep profiles.json valid JSON at all times. Only ADD to existing profile strings, never remove content.
421: PROMPT_HEADER
422:     )
423: 
424:     # Append the phase-specific context
425:     assessment_prompt="${assessment_prompt}
426: 
427: ## Phase: ${phase_id}
428: 
429: Read ${PRD_REL} implementationOrder[\"${phase_id}\"] for the story list, then proceed with the analysis above."
430: 
431:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
432:     if echo "$assessment_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1 | tee "$assessment_log"; then
433:         success "Pre-phase assessment completed for '$phase_id'"
434:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "pre_phase_assessment" "Pre-phase assessment completed" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
435:         # Validate profiles.json is still valid JSON
436:         if ! jq empty "$profiles_file" 2>/dev/null; then
437:             error "Pre-phase assessment corrupted profiles.json! Restoring backup."
438:             cp "$profiles_backup" "$profiles_file"
439:             return 1
440:         fi
441:     else
442:         warning "Pre-phase assessment failed for '$phase_id' (non-critical, continuing)"
443:     fi

454: run_hybrid_precoordination() {

457:     local coord_prompt

461: You are the coordination agent running in HYBRID PRE-PHASE mode for phase: ${phase_id}.
462: 
463: ## Task
464: 1. Read ${PRD_REL} and locate all stories in implementationOrder["${phase_id}"].
465: 2. Identify cross-lane dependencies between main, primary, and independent agent groups.
466: 3. Flag any stories where estimatedHours >= 6 or dependencies count >= 2 — these require plan mode.
467: 4. For each cross-lane dependency or plan-mode story, append a JSON message to orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl.
468:    Use this schema (one compact JSON line per message):
469:    {"id":"coord_<storyid>_<epoch>","timestamp":"<ISO8601>","from_agent":"coordination-agent","to_agent":"<agentRole>","story_id":"<id>","phase_id":"${phase_id}","message_type":"<handoff|plan_required|risk>","priority":"normal","subject":"<subject>","body":"<body>","status":"new"}

471: 6. Use: (flock -w 10 9 >> orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl; printf '%s\n' '<json>' >&9) 9>>orchestrations/logs/agent-messages.jsonl for atomic writes.

478:     )

541: INDEPENDENT_PID=""

603: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
604: run_phase_assessment() {
605:     local phase_id=$1
606:     local cost_file="$LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl"
607:     local assessment_file="$LOG_DIR/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl"
608:     local improvement_dir="$LOG_DIR/phase-improvements"

610:     mkdir -p "$improvement_dir"

612:     # Check if phase-cost.jsonl has records for this phase
613:     if [ ! -s "$cost_file" ]; then
614:         warning "No cost records found in $cost_file — skipping assessment"
615:         return 0
616:     fi
617: 
618:     local phase_records=$(grep -c "\"phase_id\":\"$phase_id\"" "$cost_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
619:     if [ "${phase_records:-0}" -eq 0 ]; then
620:         warning "No cost records for phase '$phase_id' — skipping assessment"
621:         return 0
622:     fi

624:     info "Found $phase_records cost records for phase '$phase_id'"
625: 
626:     # Build assessment prompt
627:     local assessment_prompt
628:     assessment_prompt=$(cat << PROMPT_EOF
629: You are the skill assessment agent. Analyze the phase cost data and produce an assessment.
630: 
631: ## Phase: $phase_id
632: 
633: ## Task
634: 1. Read orchestrations/logs/phase-cost.jsonl and filter for phase_id="$phase_id"
635: 2. For each task, compare elapsed_minutes vs forecast_hours (converted to minutes)
636: 3. Calculate phase-level totals and variance
637: 4. Write a single-line JSON assessment to orchestrations/logs/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl with fields:
638:    phase_id, phase_name, actual_minutes, forecast_minutes, actual_cost_usd, forecast_cost_usd,
639:    variance_minutes, variance_cost_usd, over_threshold (bool), agent_recommendations (array), notes
640: 5. Write a human-readable improvement report to orchestrations/logs/phase-improvements/${phase_id}.md
641: 6. CORRECTIVE ACTION: If any task's description clearly requires a different skill domain than the assigned agentRole,
642:    update ${PRD_REL} to change agentRole for FUTURE phase stories that have the same mismatch.
643:    - Only modify stories that are status "pending" and completed false
644:    - Only modify stories in phases AFTER the current phase (do NOT modify completed phase stories)
645:    - When changing agentRole, preserve the original value in the "originalAgentRole" field (already present)
646:    - Document every role change in the improvement report
647:    - Skill domain indicators: "TypeScript" / "Node.js" / "CLI" → backend-engineer,
648:      "React" / "UI" / "frontend" → frontend-engineer, "Docker" / "infrastructure" → devops-engineer,
649:      "Vitest" / "testing" / "E2E" → qa-engineer
650: 
651: Use flock when appending to JSONL files. If all tasks were within forecast, note "No improvements needed."
652: PROMPT_EOF
653:     )
654: 
655:     log "Running assessment agent for phase '$phase_id'..."
656:     local assessment_log="$LOG_DIR/assessment-${phase_id}.log"
657: 
658:     # Backup prd.json before assessment modifies it
659:     cp "$PRD_FILE" "${PRD_FILE}.pre-assessment"
660:     log "Backed up prd.json to ${PRD_FILE}.pre-assessment"
661: 
662:     cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
663:     if echo "$assessment_prompt" | env -u CLAUDECODE "$CLAUDE_CMD" --dangerously-skip-permissions 2>&1 | tee "$assessment_log"; then
664:         success "Phase assessment completed for '$phase_id'"
665:     else
666:         warning "Phase assessment failed for '$phase_id' (non-critical)"
667:     fi
668: }
669: 
670: # Only run assessment if cost tracking data exists
671: if [ -s "$LOG_DIR/phase-cost.jsonl" ]; then
672:     log "Step 3.5: Running post-parallel skill assessment..."
673:     run_phase_assessment "$PHASE"
674: else
675:     info "Step 3.5: No cost data yet — skipping post-parallel assessment"

678: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────

691:     '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
692:      [.stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id) != null) |
693:       select(.reviewStatus == "escalated")] | length' \
694:     "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
695: if [ "${_escalated:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
696:     error "Step 3.6: $_escalated escalated story/stories — max review iterations exhausted without approval"
697:     error "         Human review required before pipeline can proceed"
698:     exit 2
699: fi

702: # Step 4: Run review stories
703: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
704: if [ -n "$review_stories" ]; then
705:     log "Step 4: Running review stories..."

707:         [ -z "$story" ] && continue
708:     "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "code_review" "Team Lead code review completed" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
709:         log "  Running review: $story"

717: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
718: # run_unit_tests_gate <phase_id>
719: # Step 4.5: Independent unit test verification.
720: # Runs vitest (unit tests) and tsc --noEmit (type check) directly.
721: # Blocks phase gate if any suite fails. Skippable with SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE=true.
722: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
723: run_unit_tests_gate() {
724:     local phase_id="$1"
725:     local gate_log="$LOG_DIR/unit-test-gate-${phase_id}.log"
726:     local failed=0
727: 
728:     if [ "${SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE:-false}" = "true" ]; then
729:         info "Step 4.5: Unit test gate skipped (SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE=true)"
730:         return 0
731:     fi
732: 
733:     local phase_has_unit_tests
734:     phase_has_unit_tests=$(jq -r --arg phase "$phase_id" \
735:         '(.implementationOrder[$phase] // []) as $ids |
736:          [.stories[] | select(.id as $id | $ids | index($id)) | select(.unitTests == true)] | length' \
737:         "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
738:     if [ "${phase_has_unit_tests:-0}" -eq 0 ]; then
739:         info "Step 4.5: No unit-test stories in phase '$phase_id' — skipping unit test gate"
740:         return 0
741:     fi
742: 
743:     log "Step 4.5: Independent unit test gate for '$phase_id'..."
744:     echo "=== Unit Test Gate: $phase_id @ $(date -Iseconds) ===" > "$gate_log"
745: 
746:     # Node.js project: run vitest + tsc
747:     if [ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/package.json" ]; then
748:         log "  Running unit tests (vitest)..."
749:         if ~/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.0/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run \
750:                 2>&1 | tee -a "$gate_log"; then
751:             success "  Unit tests passed (vitest)"
752:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_pass" \
753:                 "Unit tests passed (vitest)" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
754:         else
755:             error "  Unit tests FAILED (vitest)"
756:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_fail" \
757:                 "Unit tests FAILED (vitest) — blocking phase gate" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
758:             failed=1
759:         fi
760: 
761:         log "  Running type check (tsc --noEmit)..."
762:         if ~/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.0/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit \
763:                 2>&1 | tee -a "$gate_log"; then
764:             success "  Type check passed (tsc)"
765:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_pass" \
766:                 "Type check passed (tsc)" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
767:         else
768:             error "  Type check FAILED (tsc)"
769:             "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "unit_test_fail" \
770:                 "Type check FAILED (tsc) — blocking phase gate" "" "main" "unit-test-runner" 2>/dev/null || true
771:             failed=1
772:         fi
773:     else
774:         info "  No package.json at PROJECT_ROOT — skipping vitest/tsc"
775:     fi
776: 
777:     echo "=== Gate Result: $([ $failed -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || echo FAIL) ===" >> "$gate_log"
778: 
779:     if [ $failed -ne 0 ]; then
780:         error "Unit test gate FAILED — fix failures and re-run this phase"
781:         error "Bypass (for non-code phases): SKIP_UNIT_TEST_GATE=true $0 --phase $phase_id"
782:         error "Log: $gate_log"
783:         return 1
784:     fi
785: 
786:     success "Unit test gate PASSED"
787:     return 0
788: }
789: 
790: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
791: # run_interstitial_e2e_phase <phase_id>
792: # Step 5.5: After phase gate passes, check for a <phase_id>_e2e phase
793: # in implementationOrder and run it. Blocks next phase if E2E fails.
794: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
795: run_interstitial_e2e_phase() {
796:     local phase_id="$1"

798: 
799:     local has_e2e_phase

801:         'if .implementationOrder[$p] then "yes" else "no" end' \
802:         "$PRD_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo "no")

804:     if [ "$has_e2e_phase" = "no" ]; then
805:         info "Step 5.5: No interstitial E2E phase for '$phase_id' — skipping"
806:         return 0
807:     fi
808: 
809:     log "Step 5.5: Running interstitial E2E phase '$e2e_phase'..."
810:     "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "e2e_gate_start" \
811:         "Starting E2E phase $e2e_phase" "" "main" "qa-engineer" 2>/dev/null || true
812: 
813:     local e2e_log="$LOG_DIR/e2e-phase-${e2e_phase}.log"
814:     if bash "$0" --phase "$e2e_phase" 2>&1 | tee "$e2e_log"; then
815:         success "Interstitial E2E phase '$e2e_phase' PASSED"
816:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "e2e_gate_pass" \
817:             "E2E phase $e2e_phase passed" "" "main" "qa-engineer" 2>/dev/null || true
818:     else
819:         local e2e_exit=$?
820:         error "Interstitial E2E phase '$e2e_phase' FAILED (exit $e2e_exit)"
821:         error "Fix E2E failures then re-run: $0 --phase $e2e_phase"
822:         error "Log: $e2e_log"
823:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "e2e_gate_fail" \
824:             "E2E phase $e2e_phase FAILED" "" "main" "qa-engineer" 2>/dev/null || true
825:         return 1
826:     fi
827: }

829: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
830: # Step 4.5: Unit test gate
831: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
832: run_unit_tests_gate "$PHASE"

834: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
835: # Step 4.8: Pre-gate worktree health verification
836: # Second chance to catch uncommitted files before gate assessment.
837: # (Step 3.1 auto-commits; this surfaces any residual issues clearly.)
838: log "Step 4.8: Pre-gate worktree verification..."
839: if ! PHASE="$PHASE" "$SCRIPT_DIR/worktree-health-check.sh" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
840:     warning "Step 4.8: Uncommitted files remain in worktrees after auto-commit — manual review recommended"
841:     warning "  Run: PHASE=$PHASE AUTO_COMMIT=true $SCRIPT_DIR/worktree-health-check.sh"
842: fi

844: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
845: # Step 5: Check phase gate
846: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
847: log "Step 5: Checking phase gate..."
848: "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_check" "Checking phase gate for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true

851: gate_result=0
852: SKIP_TESTS=true "$SCRIPT_DIR/check-phase-gate.sh" "$PHASE" 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/phase-gate-${PHASE}.log" || gate_result=$?
853: 
854: case $gate_result in
855:     0)
856:         success "Phase gate: GO - All criteria passed"
857:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_pass" "Phase gate passed for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
858:         # Step 5.5: Interstitial E2E phase (runs <PHASE>_e2e if it exists)
859:         run_interstitial_e2e_phase "$PHASE"
860:         ;;
861:     1)
862:         warning "Phase gate: RETRY - Issues found but fixable"
863:         warning "Check log for details: $LOG_DIR/phase-gate-${PHASE}.log"
864:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_retry" "Phase gate requires retry for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
865:         error "Pipeline blocked — fix issues then re-run this phase"
866:         exit 1
867:         ;;
868:     2)
869:         error "Phase gate: ESCALATE - Variance exceeds GATE_ESCALATE_THRESHOLD (${GATE_ESCALATE_THRESHOLD:-150}%)"
870:         error "Check log for details: $LOG_DIR/phase-gate-${PHASE}.log"
871:         error "Override: GATE_ESCALATE_THRESHOLD=200 $0 --phase NEXT_PHASE"
872:         "$SCRIPT_DIR/update-monitor.sh" event "phase_gate_escalate" "Phase gate requires escalation for $PHASE" "" "main" "team-lead-agent" 2>/dev/null || true
873:         exit 2
874:         ;;

878: # Step 6: Final Post-Phase Assessment
879: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────

885: fi

887: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────

889: # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
890: LOAD_GRAPH_SH="$SCRIPT_DIR/load-phase-graph.sh"

906: echo -e "${MAGENTA}============================================${NC}"

922: [ -f "$LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log" ] && info "  Independent: $LOG_DIR/wt-independent.log"
923: info "  Claude outputs: $LOG_DIR/claude_outputs/"

926: [ -s "$LOG_DIR/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl" ] && info "  Assessments: $LOG_DIR/phase-skill-assessments.jsonl"
927:

<content>1: #!/bin/bash
2: 
3: # EPAM CLI Orchestration Script - AI-driven development loop
4: # This script orchestrates Claude Code CLI for autonomous story implementation
5: #
6: # Usage:
7: #   ./claude.sh                      # Implement next stories (priority order)
8: #   ./claude.sh --phase phase1       # Implement all stories in a phase
9: #   ./claude.sh --list-phases        # Show available phases
10: #   ./claude.sh US-001 US-002        # Implement specific stories
11: #   ./claude.sh --dry-run            # Show what would be implemented
12: #   ./claude.sh --status             # Show current PRD status
13: #   ./claude.sh --interactive        # Run with permission prompts (safer)
